examples.title: Template Function - Maximum
examples.description: A simple template function to find maximum of two values of any data type
examples.explanation: The template function 'maximum' works with any type that supports the > operator. The compiler generates specific versions for int, double, and char at compile time.

examples.title: Class Template - Stack
examples.description: A generic stack class template with push, pop, and display operations
examples.explanation: The Stack class is parameterized by type T, allowing it to store any data type. The vector container manages elements internally. push() adds elements, pop() removes from top, top() returns top element.
examples.timeComplexity: push: O(1) amortized, pop: O(1), top: O(1)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of elements

examples.title: Template Specialization - Pair Class
examples.description: Class template with multiple type parameters and full specialization for char type
examples.explanation: This demonstrates primary template with two type parameters and full specialization. When Pair<char, char> is used, the specialized version displays ASCII values. For other types, the primary template is used.

examples.title: Non-type Template Parameter - Fixed Array
examples.description: Class template using non-type parameter for fixed-size array
examples.explanation: This demonstrates non-type template parameters. The size is a compile-time constant specified in angle brackets. FixedArray<int, 5> creates an array that can hold 5 integers. The compiler generates efficient code with no runtime size checking.
examples.timeComplexity: add: O(1), display: O(n) where n is current size
